changes_summary: Revision addresses four significant flags: (1) FLAG-001: all overhang math now explicitly accounts for clip speed — `overhangTimelineSeconds = overhangSource / speed`, and `getMediaEndFraction` uses timeline durations; test cases added for speed=2 and speed=0.5. (2) FLAG-002: context menu gate expanded so pinned-group clips with overhang can open the menu; trim/hold actions placed outside the `!isInPinnedShotGroup` wrapper. (3) FLAG-003: dropped the auto-clamp watcher (old Step 4) entirely — no background retry infrastructure exists, and building one is unjustified scope. Instead, added a single retry at drop time and rely on the visual indicator for remaining cases. (4) FLAG-004: removed intent-tracking requirement since auto-clamp was dropped — no need to distinguish accidental vs intentional overhang. Also chose the two-clip approach for "Hold last frame" (video + hold clip) instead of leaving it as an open design decision, since it reuses existing infrastructure.
questions: For 'Hold last frame' two-clip approach: should the new hold clip be added to the same pinned shot group as the video clip, or remain outside the group? Adding it to the group preserves contiguity management but may complicate group-level operations like delete-group.

When the overhang indicator shows on a clip whose duration was guessed (5s fallback), and the user neither trims nor holds — should there be any periodic attempt to re-resolve the duration? Current plan says no (rely on indicator + manual action), but this means some clips may permanently show the warning even if the video URL is now accessible.

success_criteria.criterion: No clip created via shot drop or switch-to-final-video has to > assetDuration when assetDuration is known at creation time
Timeline visually distinguishes the overhang zone (dead time) from playable media with a stripe/overlay pattern
Right-click context menu on an overhang clip (including pinned-group clips) offers 'Trim to media end' that correctly shortens the clip
Right-click context menu on an overhang clip offers 'Hold last frame' that splits into video + hold clip
Overhang detection and mediaEndFraction calculations correctly account for clip speed (speed != 1)
Context menu opens for pinned-shot-group clips that have overhang, while remaining restricted for non-overhang pinned clips
Existing clips without overhang are completely unaffected — no visual changes, no duration changes, no new menu items
All existing tests continue to pass (updated where necessary for new clamping or retry behavior)
Unit tests cover overhang detection with speed=1, speed>1, speed<1, hold clips, and missing duration
Overhang indicator is consistent with existing design language (amber tones matching resize clamp indicator)
Shot drop includes a single retry for duration resolution before falling back to 5 seconds
The overhang zone shows a tooltip on hover explaining the dead time and available actions
New overhang.ts utility is under 100 lines with no dependencies beyond types and config-utils
Manual smoke tests pass: drop with known duration, drop with failed duration, switch-to-final-video, trim action on pinned-group clip, hold action creates video+hold pair

assumptions: The primary cause of dead time is the 5-second fallback when video duration is unknown at drop time. A single retry at drop time will cover most transient failures; persistent failures are handled by the visual indicator.
The `hold` clip type and property already work correctly for intentional freeze frames — the two-clip approach (video + hold) reuses this without new renderer support.
Resize on visual clips adjusts speed rather than extending to, so resize is not a primary source of overhang (confirmed from useClipResize.ts:130-143).

The existing context menu in ClipAction is the right place for trim/hold actions. Expanding the canOpenContextMenu gate for overhang clips in pinned groups is safe — other menu items remain behind the !isInPinnedShotGroup guard.

Dropping the auto-clamp watcher is acceptable because: (a) no background retry system exists, (b) visual indicator + explicit actions satisfy the user's original request, (c) building reactive infrastructure for a rare edge case is disproportionate.
When assetDuration is unknown (null/undefined), the overhang detector returns no-overhang. We do not warn about unknown durations — only about confirmed overhang.
